# Personal Corpus — Reading & Writing Intelligence

Aidan's intellectual-history corpus: what he's read (Goodreads) and what he's
written (Dropbox `/Writing` + Substack). Local JSON here is the source of truth +
backup; the **Notion** databases are the delivery layer Claude reads from web/mobile.

## Files (regenerated by the scripts; safe to delete and re-pull)

| File | Source | Pulled by |
|---|---|---|
| `goodreads_reads.json` | Goodreads "read" shelf (public RSS, no login) | `scripts/personal_corpus/goodreads_fetch.py` |
| `substack_posts.json` | aidanjude.substack.com (public archive API) | `scripts/personal_corpus/substack_export.py` |
| `writing_corpus.json` | Dropbox `/Writing` (API; files are online-only placeholders locally) | `scripts/personal_corpus/dropbox_writing_pull.py` |

## Notion databases (under the "Journal Index" page, created 2026-06-04)

- **reading_library** — `c06f03ec-b284-43cc-807a-6dc775c23b64` — 310 dated reads (2014→).
- **writing_library** — `5d2055b9-74dd-4bf4-bb96-7ce211b898be` — substack + poetry +
  prose + journals + novel index (the substack/Dropbox rows were added 2026-06-04).

## Refresh everything

```bash
export $(grep -v ^# .env | xargs)
python3 scripts/personal_corpus/goodreads_fetch.py
python3 scripts/personal_corpus/substack_export.py
python3 scripts/personal_corpus/dropbox_writing_pull.py
python3 scripts/personal_corpus/notion_push.py --all   # needs NOTION_TOKEN; dedups
```

Or run the **/corpus-refresh** skill, which does all of the above. See also
`/goodreads-fetch`, `/substack-export`, `/intellectual-history-analysis`.

## Gotchas

- Goodreads blocks unauthenticated HTML scraping (redirects to sign-in). The RSS
  feed is the way in. Only ~310 of ~480 shelved books have real read-dates; the
  rest are undated backlog.
- Dropbox `/Writing` files are **online-only placeholders** (0 bytes on disk) and
  a 2024-12-31 bulk import flattened their local dates — always pull content +
  `client_modified` via the Dropbox API, never off disk.
